title: Questions from Quarantine: Good News for the Future
summary: The Security and Global Health Project is proud to present a weekly web series with Security Mom Juliette Kayyem and Medicine Mom Dr. Margaret Bourdeaux. Each week, our experts will answer your questions from quarantine and give you advice on staying sane and sanitary in a global crisis. We hope you'll join our Moms every Tuesday, and if you have a question that you want answered, tweet with #QuestionsFromQuarantine.
body: The Security and Global Health Project is proud to present a weekly web series with Security Mom Juliette Kayyem and Medicine Mom Dr. Margaret Bourdeaux. Each week, our experts will answer your questions from quarantine and give you advice on staying sane and sanitary in a global crisis. We hope you'll join our Moms every Tuesday, and if you have a question that you want answered, tweet with #QuestionsFromQuarantine . _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: The article introduces a weekly web series titled 'Questions from Quarantine,' featuring experts Juliette Kayyem and Dr. Margaret Bourdeaux, who provide advice on managing mental and physical well-being during a global health crisis. It invites the public to submit questions via social media using a specific hashtag and promotes regular Tuesday sessions. The content is framed within the context of the Security and Global Health Project, highlighting its role in offering guidance during the coronavirus pandemic. The piece also references related expert analyses on topics such as mega-event safety, supply chains, and potential migration crises, positioning the series as part of a broader discussion on global health and security policy.
result.main_content_zh: 本文介绍了一个名为“隔离问题”的每周网络系列节目，由专家朱丽叶·凯耶姆和玛格丽特·布尔多博士主持，旨在为全球健康危机期间的心理和生理健康管理提供建议。文章邀请公众通过特定标签在社交媒体上提问，并宣传每周二的定期会议。内容被置于“安全与全球健康项目”的框架内，强调其在冠状病毒大流行期间提供指导的作用。文章还引用了相关专家对大型活动安全、供应链和潜在移民危机等主题的分析，将该系列定位为关于全球健康与安全政策更广泛讨论的一部分。
